notes: High automatic pose and shoulder rates were misleading; full-video review showed unstable and anatomically incorrect skeletons especially at distance; phone-anchor rates were 0.2299/0.7273/0.1716 but did not imply anatomical correctness
Corrected IMAGE-mode smoke still produced only 0.2687 seatbelt-anchor and 0.2015 phone-anchor rates with a 91-frame miss run; accepted frames remained anatomically unstable in manual overlay review

Better face-shoulder-torso continuity than prior models but full-video review found recurring arm disappearance and bent phone-arm instability; per-video phone arm-chain rates were 0.1070/0.8134/0.2015 so it cannot support the common phone-smoking action pose contract

Corrected three-video run improved over RTMPose but manual review still found unstable and inconsistent upper-body hand pose; video_3 hand anchor 0.2388 and hand-near-face 0.0746 are not enough for phone or smoking anchors while video_2 near-perfect rates are visually over-optimistic; keep as fine-tune candidate only

Selected only for driver shoulder-torso continuity and seatbelt ROI anchoring; poor frames with YuNet face confidence >=0.80 run evidence-only and remain outside risk decisions; arm and hand anchors are disabled because elbow-wrist motion was not anatomically stable enough for phone or smoking decisions
